message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_06-41-11_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "3 runes yield 60 G base premium (block applies)"
Expected: 71 G (60 block + 6 first ins + 5 fee)
Current error: Expected 71, received 88
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_06-41-11_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Implement: when there are exactly 3 components of the SAME type, treat them as a block with base premium 60 G (instead of 75). For now, the simplest: count components by type; if any type has exactly 3 components, that group costs 60 instead of 75. Otherwise individual 25 each.

Make all tests pass. Don't worry about other block tests yet.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement minimal code to make it pass.

Now I understand. Currently 3 runes = 25*3 = 75. With first insurance: 75 * 1.1 = 82.5, plus fee 5 = 87.5, ceil = 88.

We need 60 + 6 + 5 = 71. So when there are exactly 3 components of the same type, the group costs 60 instead of 75.

Minimal implementation: count components by type, if any type has exactly 3, that group costs 60 instead of 25*3.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added component grouping by type; when a component type has exactly 3 items, the group costs 60 G (block discount) instead of 75 G (3 × 25 G). Otherwise, components are billed individually at 25 G each.
**Result**: All tests now pass (7 passing, 29 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal - simply counts components by type, applies block price (60) only when count is exactly 3. The block check is hardcoded to exactly 3 with hardcoded value 60, leaving generalization for future tests.
